# Disable automatic renewal

This tutorial explains how to disable automatic renewal for your subscription by placing a configuration order.

### Prerequisites <a href="#howtodownsizeamicrosoft365subscriptionlicense-prerequisites" id="howtodownsizeamicrosoft365subscriptionlicense-prerequisites"></a>

Ensure that both the subscription and its associated agreement are active.&#x20;

### Disable automatic renewal

{% stepper %}
{% step %}
**Start the process**

To start the process:

1. Go to **Marketplace** > **Subscriptions**.
2. Select the required subscription.&#x20;
3. On the subscription details page, select the dropdown arrow <img src="data:image/png;base64," alt="<svg xmlns=&#x22;http://www.w3.org/2000/svg&#x22; height=&#x22;24px&#x22; viewBox=&#x22;0 -960 960 960&#x22; width=&#x22;24px&#x22; fill=&#x22;#5f6368&#x22;><path d=&#x22;M480-344 240-584l56-56 184 184 184-184 56 56-240 240Z&#x22;/></svg>" data-size="line">, then choose **Configure**.&#x20;
{% endstep %}

{% step %}
**Submit the configuration order**

In the guided configuration workflow, do the following:

1. **Auto-renewal** – Select **Disabled**, then select **Next**.&#x20;
2. **Order details** – Add any reference details or comments, then select **Next**.
3. **Review order** – Review the order details, then select **Place order**.&#x20;
4. **Summary** – Select **View order** to open the order details page or select **Close**.
{% endstep %}
{% endstepper %}

### Next steps

After the configuration order is submitted, your subscription will remain active through the end of the current commitment period and will then expire without any additional charges.
